expectations.text: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) — asks clarifying questions about the auth system before decomposing
expectations.evidence: Step 6 Phase 0: Design gate triggered. Trigger evaluation identifies '3+ distinct features: registration, login, password reset (YES)', 'external integrations: JWT tokens, email notification, password hashing (YES)', and 'Ambiguities present (YES)'. Three clarifying questions asked before decomposition in Step 8.

expectations.text: Produces a design summary with components, data flow, and key decisions before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Step 0c produces a formal design summary with sections: Components (UserRegistration, UserLogin, PasswordReset), Data Flow (Registration, Login, Password Reset flows), Key Decisions (JWT with refresh tokens, bcrypt, lockout rules, etc.), Out of Scope. Summary stored in state under designSummary.

expectations.text: Decomposes into at least 3 work units (registration, login, password-reset or similar)
expectations.evidence: Step 8 Phase 2 produces exactly 3 work units: Unit 1 (user-registration), Unit 2 (user-login), Unit 3 (password-reset). Each has its own spec-contract, test files, impl files, and dependency declarations.

expectations.text: Identifies dependencies between units (login depends on registration, password-reset depends on registration)
expectations.evidence: Unit 2 (user-login): dependsOn=[user-registration]. Unit 3 (password-reset): dependsOn=[user-registration]. Unit 1 (user-registration): dependsOn=[]. Dependencies explicitly declared and reflected in DAG.

expectations.text: Respects dependency order — implements registration before login and password-reset
expectations.evidence: Step 10: Ready Queue starts with [user-registration] only. Registration runs first (Slot 1). After registration COMPLETES, both user-login and password-reset are added to ready queue and dispatched. Execution plan shows: 'Ready immediately: user-registration. After user-registration: user-login, password-reset (parallel).'

expectations.text: Each work unit goes through the full RED->GREEN->review cycle independently
expectations.evidence: All three units independently execute: Test Writer (4a) -> RED Verification (4b) -> Code Writer (4c) -> GREEN Verification (4d) -> Spec Compliance (4e) -> Adversarial (4f) -> Code Quality (4g). Each unit has its own checksums, test files, and review verdicts.

expectations.text: Runs a final integration test across all units after individual completion
expectations.evidence: Step 11 Phase 5: 'Run FULL test suite: npx vitest run. All tests across 3 test files pass.' Plus: 'Holistic review: check that user-login and password-reset correctly interface with user-registration's user store/types.' And: 'Cross-unit integration: verify login works with registered users, password-reset works with registered users.'

expectations.text: The report shows all work units with their individual phase statuses
expectations.evidence: Step 12 Phase 6: 'Generate tdd-report.md: 3/3 units completed, ~25-30 total tests, ~30-40 total assertions, 0 anti-cheat violations, All reviews passed, Model assignments recorded per unit.' Report structure includes per-unit tracking.

expectations.text: Assigns model and effort per agent based on complexity — opus for architecture-sensitive Test Writers and reviewers, sonnet for Code Writers
expectations.evidence: Step 8 Model Assignment table shows: user-registration (Integration) gets sonnet/high for all roles. user-login and password-reset (Architecture) get opus/high for Test Writer, Spec Compliance, and Adversarial reviewers, while Code Writer and Code Quality get sonnet/high. This matches the expected pattern.

claims: Design gate triggers correctly for multi-component auth system with security concerns
Clarifying questions asked one at a time (Q1, Q2, Q3 sequentially) with trade-off proposals
Dependency DAG correctly computed: registration is root, login and password-reset depend on it
Eager dispatch demonstrated: login and password-reset dispatched in parallel after registration completes
Mixed model assignment: opus/high for architecture-sensitive agents, sonnet/high for mechanical agents
Full three-stage review (spec compliance, adversarial, code quality) runs independently per unit

suggestions: The trace assumes the auth system integration tests pass, but does not show what happens if cross-unit integration fails (e.g., type mismatch between registration output and login input). A failure path variant would be valuable.
Consider adding an assertion about the design summary being passed downstream to Test Writers and reviewers, which is a key skill feature mentioned in the summary but not explicitly asserted.
eval_feedback.overall: The with_skill run demonstrates the full complexity of the TDD skill pipeline for a multi-component system. All 9 assertions pass. The design gate, dependency analysis, eager dispatch, mixed model assignment, and per-unit TDD pipeline are all correctly exercised. The auth system eval is the most thorough test of the skill's orchestration capabilities.
